title: Cone Object
description: Transient cone. A transient cone is not displayed or saved in a document. A transient cone is used as a wrapper to work with raw cone information. A transient cone has no boundaries. The cone always goes to a point in its narrowing direction, and is infinite in its widening direction. They are created statically using the create method of the Cone class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Cone object.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets the data that defines the cone.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets the data that defines the cone.

methods.name: transformBy
methods.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.

properties.name: axis
properties.description: Gets and sets the center axis (along the length) of the cone that defines its normal direction.

properties.name: halfAngle
properties.description: Gets and sets the taper half-angle of the cone in radians. A negative value indicates that the cone is narrowing in the direction of the axis vector, whereas a positive value indicates that it is expanding in the direction of the axis vector.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Gets and sets the origin point (center) of the base of the cone.

properties.name: radius
properties.description: Gets and sets the radius of the cone.

axis.title: Cone.axis Property
axis.description: Gets and sets the center axis (along the length) of the cone that defines its normal direction.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cone_var.axis

# Set the value of the property.
cone_var.axis = propertyValue

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cone_var.evaluator

halfAngle.title: Cone.halfAngle Property
halfAngle.description: Gets and sets the taper half-angle of the cone in radians. A negative value indicates that the cone is narrowing in the direction of the axis vector, whereas a positive value indicates that it is expanding in the direction of the axis vector.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cone_var.halfAngle

# Set the value of the property.
cone_var.halfAngle = propertyValue

isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cone_var.isValid

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

syntax.python:

radius.title: Cone.radius Property
radius.description: Gets and sets the radius of the cone.

syntax.python:

propertyValue = cone_var.radius

# Set the value of the property.
cone_var.radius = propertyValue

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cone_var.surfaceType

classType.title: Cone.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Cone.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Cone object.
copy.return_description: Returns a new Cone object that is a copy of this Cone object.

create.title: Cone.create Method
create.return_description: Returns the new Cone object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point (center) of the base of the cone.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The center axis (along the length) of the cone that defines its normal direction.

parameters.name: halfAngle
parameters.description: The taper half-angle of the cone.

getData.title: Cone.getData Method
getData.description: Gets the data that defines the cone.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The output origin point (center) of the base of the cone.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The output center axis (along the length) of the cone that defines its normal direction.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The output radius of the cone.

parameters.name: halfAngle
parameters.description: The output taper half-angle of the cone.

set.title: Cone.set Method
set.description: Sets the data that defines the cone.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point (center) of the base of the cone.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The center axis (along the length) of the cone that defines its normal direction.

parameters.name: halfAngle
parameters.description: The taper half-angle of the cone.

transformBy.title: Cone.transformBy Method
transformBy.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.
transformBy.return_description: Returns true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 3D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the surface.
